_comment: Component healthy (normal) and faulty (anomalous) multiplier bands on the nominal value (1.0 = nominal). Grounded in docs/REFERENCES.md ('Component Tolerances & Degradation Ranges'). 'anomalous': null marks an operating-point knob (not a fault). 'normal_step'/'anomalous_step' are the additive multiplier steps the generator uses to sample each band (fine for the narrow tolerance band, coarse for the wide degradation band); omit a step to fall back to --normal-levels/--fault-levels. Edit / copy this file per converter (data/<converter>/component_ranges.json).

Esr_C.note: cold-temp ESR ~2-3x is reversible/healthy; fault = ESR >= 3x (IEC 60384-4-1 wear-out)

Lout.note: ±20% tol + Isat roll-off; fault = saturation (L drop)

Esr_L.note: copper tempco normal; large rise = winding degradation

Rout.note: operating point, not a fault; excluded from the tolerance sweep
